lines.speaker: Dodgy Character
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You look a bit shady, if I do say so myself.

lines.speaker: Dodgy Character
lines.text: Well that's the nicest thing anyone has ever said to me!

lines.speaker: Oriana
lines.text: The name's Oriana. Nice to meet ya!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey, I saw that!

lines.speaker: Oriana
lines.text: Sorry... Force of 'abit.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right... Well I'm [player name]. What is it you do around here?

lines.speaker: Oriana
lines.text: Well I'm the best thief to ever live, ain't I.

lines.speaker: Oriana
lines.text: This place is filled to the brim with wealthy folk with lots of pricey stuff. It's all prime for the takin'.

lines.speaker: Oriana
lines.text: Now what can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know what these keys are for?

lines.speaker: Oriana
lines.text: Let me take a look.

lines.speaker: Oriana
lines.text: Those are keys to the houses round 'ere.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, so I can just waltz right in?

lines.speaker: Oriana
lines.text: Essentially yes! Though the keys you showed me look a bit old. I'd try to find as many as you can just in case they break.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you tell me more about these rich folk?

lines.speaker: Oriana
lines.text: Yes I can. Some 'ave got houses full of loot, while others spend their days at the bazaar. What do you want to know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about the houses.

lines.speaker: Oriana
lines.text: Some of them round here are filled with loot for sure. They've got so many bits and bobs, trinkets and doodads, that they 'ardly ever notice they've been robbed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You rob them?

lines.speaker: Oriana
lines.text: Oh yes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do you manage that?

lines.speaker: Oriana
lines.text: First we get keys for these houses, which are always conveniently under the doormat.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you serious? It's that easy?

lines.speaker: Oriana
lines.text: Ha... No. I was just pulling your leg.

lines.speaker: Oriana
lines.text: You'll 'ave to steal the house keys from the citizens.

lines.speaker: Oriana
lines.text: All these wealthy folk buy and sell property like it's nothing, so they 'ave plenty of spare keys to go round.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So how do you steal them?

lines.speaker: Oriana
lines.text: We pick their pockets of course. I 'ave some of my crew in the bazaar. If you fancy gettin' involved, they might help you out every now and then.

lines.speaker: Oriana
lines.text: Once you 'ave a key, you can break into a house. Not any ol' house though. It must be one that's ready to be robbed.

lines.speaker: Oriana
lines.text: The street urchins that roam the streets keep tabs on which houses are vulnerable.

lines.speaker: Oriana
lines.text: Once you know a house is vulnerable, wait for the owner to leave. Then just simply use your key to get in and steal to your heart's content!

lines.speaker: Oriana
lines.text: But whatever you do, dont get caught!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So... steal keys, use keys on the houses that are ready to be robbed, make buckets of cash and don't be caught?

lines.speaker: Oriana
lines.text: That's it!

lines.speaker: Oriana
lines.text: If you're not sure where to begin, chat to the urchins in my crew posted round the houses. They'll help you find the next one to plunder.

lines.speaker: Oriana
lines.text: Though it might come at a price.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about the bazaar.

lines.speaker: Oriana
lines.text: Some of the wealthiest citizens in Varlamore shop in the bazaar and nearby. A perfect place to pick some pockets!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Surely it's not so easy though?

lines.speaker: Oriana
lines.text: You'd be right about that. If it were easy to steal from 'em, they wouldn't be so rich, would they?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds like you might have some tips?

lines.speaker: Oriana
lines.text: Some of the street urchins in my crew hang around in the bazaar and on the outskirts. They might be able to provide some sort of distraction.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: They do it just out of the goodness of their heart?

lines.speaker: Oriana
lines.text: You're funny, [player name].

lines.speaker: Oriana
lines.text: They get a cut in all the wealth we make.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I get a cut?

lines.speaker: Oriana
lines.text: Cheeky! You're lucky I don't steal from you right now!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd better get going.

lines.speaker: Oriana
lines.text: See you later.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you work alone?

lines.speaker: Oriana
lines.text: Nah! I 'ave a network of spies and pickpockets that roam the city. You might've seen 'em?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Seen them? Not sure I have.

lines.speaker: Oriana
lines.text: Hidden in plain sight. Perfect! Well it's all the street urchins of course. You can tell if they are part of my crew by the purple sash around their waist.

lines.speaker: Oriana
lines.text: Most of them lost their parents to the Colosseum, hoping to get glory for their family. The Colosseum 'owever, had other plans...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's awful.

lines.speaker: Oriana
lines.text: It really is. They had nowhere to go and no food to eat, so I offered them a home, food, and all the gold they would ever need to 'ave a good life.

lines.speaker: Oriana
lines.text: No one suspects them, which makes them perfect spies.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can they help me in anyway?

lines.speaker: Oriana
lines.text: Yes they can! Some in the residential area will point out houses free to rob, and some will be in and around the bazaar, distracting the wealthy citizens.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well that's handy.

lines.speaker: Oriana
lines.text: It sure is, but give them any trouble, and I'll throw you in the Colosseum myself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Understood.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's your story?

lines.speaker: Oriana
lines.text: Not much to tell really.

lines.speaker: Oriana
lines.text: I was an orphan, much like these street urchins you see around the city. I spent my days running around scrounging for food and struggling to find somewhere to sleep.

lines.speaker: Oriana
lines.text: Eventually, I turned to a life of crime and thievery. Turns out I'm quite good at it.

lines.speaker: Oriana
lines.text: I took refuge in a small spot in the north east of the city. Out of sight, out of mind.

lines.speaker: Oriana
lines.text: There I met with other orphans just like me. We all wanted payback. Payback on the city for forgetting about us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sure there are better places for you and your crew to find refuge outside of the city. Even outside of Varlamore.

lines.speaker: Oriana
lines.text: That may be the case, [player name], but I can't in good conscience leave any of the folk here who are struggling. I swore to help anyone in need.

lines.speaker: Oriana
lines.text: While the Colosseum still stands, family greed for glory will cost people. Some just their livelihoods, others their lives!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you really want to make a difference?

lines.speaker: Oriana
lines.text: Well don't get me wrong, I still want to get filthy stinking rich as well!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't we all...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd better get going.

lines.speaker: Oriana
lines.text: See you later.

sections.title: After stealing valuables for the first time

lines.speaker: Oriana
lines.text: Managed to steal anything yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have actually! I've found these valuables from some of the houses.

lines.speaker: Oriana
lines.text: Want some coin for those? I'll give you a good deal... One coin a piece.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: One measly coin? That hardly seems worth it.

lines.speaker: Oriana
lines.text: The thing is, you won't be able to sell those yourself, making them worthless. Still, how 'bout five coins a piece?

lines.speaker: Oriana
lines.text: That's crazy! Ever 'eard of inflation. It affects us thieves too...

lines.speaker: Oriana
lines.text: 30 coins. No higher.

lines.speaker: Oriana
lines.text: 55 coins is my final offer.

lines.speaker: Oriana
lines.text: 65 coins is my final offer. I've even throw in a little extra as you seem to be the talk of the town, [rank].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I suppose that's better than nothing.

lines.speaker: Oriana
lines.text: Good. Do you want to trade them in now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sure thing.

lines.speaker: Oriana
lines.text: There ya go! Don't spend it all at once.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not at the moment.

lines.speaker: Oriana
lines.text: Suit yourself. What to chat 'bout something else?

lines.speaker: Oriana
lines.text: How's it going, [Player name]?

sections.title: If trading valuables to Oriana after starting

lines.speaker: Oriana
lines.text: There ya go! Don't spend it all at once.

sections.title: If trading in nothing to Oriana

lines.speaker: Oriana
lines.text: You don't seem to anything to trade in to me.
